title: Ensure that the --profiling argument is set to false

input_row.title: Ensure that the --profiling argument is set to false
input_row.rationale: Profiling allows for the identification of specific performance bottlenecks. It generates a significant amount of program data that could potentially be exploited to uncover system and program details. If you are not experiencing any bottlenecks and do not need the profiler for troubleshooting purposes, it is recommended to turn it off to reduce the potential attack surface. Impact: Profiling information would not be available.
input_row.audit: Run the following command on the Control Plane node: ps -ef | grep kube-controller-manager Verify that the --profiling argument is set to false.
input_row.remediation: Edit the Controller Manager pod specification file /etc/kubernetes/manifests/kube- controller-manager.yaml on the Control Plane node and set the below parameter. --profiling=false Default Value: By default, profiling is enabled. References: 1. https://kubernetes.io/docs/admin/kube-controller-manager/ 2. https://github.com/kubernetes/community/blob/master/contributors/devel/profiling. md
input_row.impact: Profiling information would not be available.

step1_initial_assessment:
**AUDIT APPROACH:** Automated

**PROGRAM NAME:** k8s_controller_manager_profiling_disabled

**AUTOMATION DETAILS:**
- Query the kube-controller-manager Pod specification, typically located in the static Pod manifest at /etc/kubernetes/manifests/kube-controller-manager.yaml, or via the Kubernetes API if the controller manager is managed as a Pod.
- Use kubectl to retrieve the kube-controller-manager Pod spec:
  - Command: kubectl -n kube-system get pod -l component=kube-controller-manager -o json
  - Alternatively, directly inspect the manifest file if running as a static Pod on the control plane node.
- Parse the command and args fields of the kube-controller-manager container to check for the presence and value of the --profiling flag.
- Pass Criteria: --profiling=false is explicitly set in the command or args for the kube-controller-manager container.
- Fail Criteria: --profiling is missing (defaults to true) or set to true.
- Example check logic:
  - For each kube-controller-manager Pod/container, confirm that --profiling=false is present in the command/args.
  - If not present or set to true, flag as non-compliant.
- This check can be fully automated using Kubernetes API calls or kubectl, as the Pod spec and container args are exposed and do not require host-level access.

**SOURCE:** CIS KUBERNETES BENCHMARK V1.11 PDF
